channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Japan launches an advanced Earth observation satellite on its new flagship H3 rocket
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan deployed an upgraded Earth observation satellite for disaster response and security after it was launched on a new flagship H3 rocket Monday. The H3 No.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine's convicts offered release at a high price: Joining the fight against Russia
item.description: DNIPROPETROVSK REGION, Ukraine (AP) — At a rural penal colony in southeast Ukraine, several convicts stand assembled under barbed wire to hear an army recruiter offer them a shot at parole. In return, they must join the grueling fight against Russia.
item.dc:creator: VASILISA STEPANENKO, EVGENIY MALOLETKA and DEREK GATOPOULOS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea test-launches 2 ballistic missiles, after end of new US-South Korea-Japan drill
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea test-fired two ballistic missile Monday but one of them possibly flew abnormally, South Korea's military said, a day after the North vowed “offensive and overwhelming” responses to a new U.S. military drill with South Korea and Japan.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In a proud and troubled UK town, voters wonder whether their election choice will make a difference
item.description: HARTLEPOOL, England (AP) — A lot of politicians have promised change to voters in Hartlepool, a wind-whipped port town in northeast England. For decades, Labour Party representatives said they would fight for working people, even as well-paid industrial jobs disappeared.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Australia appoints second woman governor-general in 123 years to represent British monarch
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australia appointed Sam Mostyn on Monday as only its second woman governor-general, a largely ceremonial role representing the British monarch who is the nation’s head of state.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: McLaughlin-Levrone sends pre-Olympic jolt running 50.65 to break world record (again) in 400 hurdles
item.description: EUGENE, Ore. (AP) — For the better part of two years, Sydney McLaughlin-Levrone all but disappeared from the 400-meter hurdles course. Turns out, she wasn't hiding or looking for something else to do. Just getting better at what she does best.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: French far-right scores convincing first-round election win: What to know
item.description: The far-right National Rally party in France scored a convincing early lead in the first round of the country’s parliamentary elections on Sunday, according to pollsters, bringing controversial leader Marine Le Pen a step closer to forming a government.
item.dc:creator: Nick Robertson, The Hill

item.title: US bases in Europe on high alert for possible terrorist attack: DOD
item.description: All U.S. military bases in Europe have been put on heightened alert status due to a potential terrorist attack, Fox News Digital has confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tourist reportedly dies under mysterious circumstances on idyllic vacation in Greece: 'Puzzling'
item.description: A 44-year-old man from the United Kingdom was found dead on the Greek island of Spetses, according to local officials. He was found with a mysterious head injury.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea launches ballistic missile off east coast, Seoul says
item.description: North Korea reportedly launched a ballistic missile off its eastern coast Monday after vowing retaliation over a U.S. military drill with South Korea and Japan.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US military in Europe on heightened terror alert
item.description: The command told U.S. personnel in Europe that condition “Charlie,” the second-highest alert level. is in effect.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Macron on edge as France’s right-wing National Rally party gains momentum in first round of elections
item.description: France’s National Rally party made considerable gains in the country’s first round of elections Sunday, putting centrist President Emmanuel Macron and his supporters on edge.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bolivian president orchestrated a 'self-coup,' political rival Evo Morales claims
item.description: Former Bolivian President Evo Morales is accusing his political ally-turned-rival President Luis Arce of deceiving the Bolivian people by staging a “self-coup” last week.
item.dc:creator: Megan Janetsky, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Female suicide bombers kill at least 18 in coordinated attack in Nigeria, authorities say
item.description: MAIDUGURI, Nigeria (AP) — Female suicide bombers targeted a wedding, a funeral and a hospital in coordinated attacks in northern Nigeria that killed at least 18 people, local authorities said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: DYEPKAZAH SHIBAYAN and HARUNA UMAR, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Storms in Switzerland and Italy cause flooding and landslides, leaving at least 4 people dead
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Storms in Switzerland and northern Italy caused extensive flooding and landslides, leaving at least four people dead, authorities said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: UN official called 'terror sympathizing antisemite' by Israeli ambassador as calls grow for her dismissal
item.description: Pressure is mounting on the United Nations to dismiss Francesca Albanese, its controversial special rapporteur on the Palestinian territories, for her anti-Israel statements.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dutch Prime Minister Mark Rutte urges support for Ukraine, EU and NATO in his farewell speech
item.description: Long-serving Dutch Prime Minister Mark Rutte has urged his country to support Ukraine and international cooperation in his final address to his compatriots.
item.dc:creator: Molly Quell, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Oldest shipwreck discovered changes theories of ancient trade
item.description: The ship can't be brought to the surface, but archaeologists have recovered ancient jugs it was carrying.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: US struggles to deter Houthi threat as crisis spirals
item.description: After half a year of conflict, the U.S. has failed to deter the Houthi rebels from attacking merchant ships in the Red Sea as the Yemeni fighters continue to sink commercial boats and disrupt global trade, posing an increasingly difficult challenge for the far larger American military. Repeated U.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Most of the world prefers Biden but is preparing for Trump
item.description: Former President Trump’s apparent victory in the presidential debate Thursday night has added urgency to efforts underway by world leaders to prepare for a second Trump administration, despite international audiences preferring President Biden.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Zelenskyy appeals to West to relax targeting limits for Ukraine as glide bombs hammer front line
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says Russia dropped more than 800 glide bombs in Ukraine in the past week alone and appealed to Western countries to further relax targeting restrictions inside Russia.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Beryl strengthens into first hurricane of 2024 Atlantic season. Here’s what you should know
item.description: Hurricane Beryl became a hurricane Saturday, the first of the 2024 Atlantic season, according to discussions from the National Hurricane Center (NHC).
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Every Israeli crime 'more egregious than the last': Gaza ICU doctor
item.description: ICU pediatrician: "We are essentially at the mercy of what Israel will and will not let in or out" of Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Urja Sinha, NewsNation

item.title: From classroom to podium: The journey of US student athletes to the Olympics
item.description: Student athletes heading to the Olympics in Paris this summer face a monumental task training for their sport while completing college courses. Universities will be sending scores of students to the games, representing the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas, The Hill

item.title: Twice-yearly injection fully protects women from HIV, trial finds
item.description: The trial showed the new injection "demonstrated 100% efficacy as a preventive treatment" for HIV among African women.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: U.S. couple among hundreds who died in pilgrimage to Mecca
item.description: A couple from Maryland were among the more than 1,300 people who died during the Hajj pilgrimage in Saudi Arabia, Democratic Senate candidate Angela Alsobrooks announced Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Nick Robertson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Apple becomes first target of EU's new digital competition rules aimed at big tech
item.description: European Union regulators are accusing Apple of breaking new rules on digital competition by imposing rules in its App Store marketplace that prevent app makers from pointing users to cheaper options on other venues.
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Princess Anne hospitalized with minor injuries after 'incident,' Buckingham Palace says
item.description: Buckingham Palace says Princess Anne has sustained minor injuries and concussion following an incident on the Gatcombe Park estate on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
